provenance.source_corpus: Assessing the Effectiveness of a Constructed Arctic Stream Using Multiple Biological Attributes

nodes.name: Initial efforts to improve fish habitat in the constructed stream focused on physical structures

source_spans.text: Because initial efforts to improve fish habitat in the constructed stream focused on physical structures (e.g., weirs, vanes, rock, groins), ecological factors limiting fish growth were not considered and likely constrained success.

Because initial efforts to improve fish habitat in the constructed stream focused on physical structures (e.g., weirs, vanes, rock, groins), ecological factors limiting fish growth were not considered and likely constrained success.

nodes.name: Species richness and densities of macroinvertebrates

source_spans.text: Species richness and densities of macroinvertebrates were consistently lower in the constructed stream compared to natural streams.
This contributed to differences in macroinvertebrate assemblage structure throughout the period
The virtual lack of local riparian vegetation to serve as a source pool for large woody debris and CPOM
combined with low retention of large woody materials
also precludes the establishment of snag-dwelling invertebrate communities
In turn, lower macroinvertebrate densities resulted in reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling
In turn, lower macroinvertebrate densities resulted in reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling
In turn, lower macroinvertebrate densities resulted in reduced forage base and growth of young-of-the-year Arctic grayling
Low macroinvertebrate abundances and low coverage by macrophytes and bryophytes in the constructed stream may also have resulted because much of it was still in a phase of hydraulic flux
Low macroinvertebrate abundances and low coverage by macrophytes and bryophytes in the constructed stream may also have resulted because much of it was still in a phase of hydraulic flux
Low macroinvertebrate abundances and low coverage by macrophytes and bryophytes in the constructed stream may also have resulted because much of it was still in a phase of hydraulic flux
